methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Marathon Fusion is a Seed-stage nuclear energy company founded by Adam Rutkowski and Kyle Schiller that has raised $10.9M to date. The nuclear energy sector comprises a peer universe of 11 companies—3 public (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private—including comparable-stage players such as Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Stage-progression analysis, the sole applicable valuation method, applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement to the $10.9M raised as a proxy for current value, yielding an implied valuation of $27M. Peer-median comparison was not feasible; while 8 same-sector private companies exist, filtering for Seed-stage or adjacent-stage peers with comparable valuation tiers produced insufficient datapoints (fewer than 3 required for statistical reliability), making sector-wide medians misleading rather than instructive.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $23M to $31M, centered on $27M. This range reflects the inherent uncertainty of single-method triangulation and the absence of narrow peer benchmarking. Key uncertainties include the company's progress toward demonstrating technical differentiation within a crowded early-stage nuclear landscape, capital efficiency relative to listed and later-stage private peers, and the extent to which downstream regulatory and commercialization milestones will drive step-up velocity to Series A and beyond.
